frameworks.name: excitatory-inhibitory recurrent neural network
frameworks.one_line: trained RNNs with separate excitatory and inhibitory units obeying Dale's principle

frameworks.name: gradient descent training
frameworks.one_line: optimization of network parameters under biological constraints to perform cognitive tasks

frameworks.name: connectionist model of cognition
frameworks.one_line: trained networks as proxies for biological circuits performing cognitive tasks

core_question: How can recurrent neural networks be trained by gradient descent while respecting biological constraints such as separate excitatory and inhibitory populations?
key_assumptions: trained RNNs are valid proxies for biological circuits
Dale's principle (sign-restricted units) is essential for biological realism
task-optimized parameters yield mechanistic hypotheses for neural computation
manipulations: imposed Dale's principle on RNN units
incorporated additional biological constraints during gradient descent training
key_findings: a simple and flexible framework can train E-I RNNs by gradient descent on cognitive tasks
biologically constrained RNNs can be optimized without sacrificing task performance
interpretations: biologically constrained trained RNNs provide a platform for mechanistic hypotheses about cognitive circuits
explicit E/I separation makes trained networks more directly comparable to neural data
